title: EndpointType::as_str/from_uri naming asymmetry may confuse future contributors
description: from_uri constructs from a URI string; as_str returns a URI string. The asymmetry (one says 'uri', one says 'str') is jarring on first read. A contributor seeing as_str() might expect a non-URI string representation. Consider either from_str/as_str or from_uri/as_uri. Current choice was deliberate (as_str follows the other Unknown-bearing enum pattern) but is undocumented.
close_reason: Intentional: from_uri names the domain input (URI), as_str names the accessor convention. Rust std uses as_str() for str-view methods; from_uri() signals a domain-aware parse not a raw string parse. Asymmetry is justified. No code change.
